pages.raw_text:
City Electrical Inspector ]. NJ.
Halliday reports having inspected
$500 jobs of electric wiring since
the opening of his department last
November This is phenomenal
run of work. as these inspections
in over 80 per cent of the cases
were on new buildings. Mr.. Halla-
day has two field assistants and
a clerk in his Office at total cost
of SIT per day. Although inspec-
lions are made for about one half
the price charged by the insurance
companies prior to the city taking
over the work, yet the department
is more than self sustaining and
will net a handsome profit to the
city.


In all probability electric light
is used in more homes in Spokane
in proportion to population than
any other city of its size in the
country. If the Washington Water
oWSI ". WtIc Ioili pCliCb W VI vi
nance to sell electric light for do
mestic use at a fair profit the use
of this form of illumination in the
homes would become well nigh
universal. The large use of cleo
trio light in Spokane homes is ac-
counted for because of the high
price of coal oil and Illuminating
gas. The element of difference in
cost between gas and oil on the
one hand and electric light on the
other is so small that people gen
orally pay it in order to secure
the additional conveniences of the
electricity.


There are about 60 concerns, big
and little, engaged in the business
of electric wiring in Spokane. and
all seem to have plenty to do. In
spector Halliday at times during
the last few months has been
showed under with work, but has
managed to get around it. The in.
spection fee runs from so cents to
$3.50 for ordinary residences.
